'''Michael Robertson''' (born 14 April 1983) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. His usual position was on the {{rlp|wg}} but he could also play as a {{rlp|fb}} with equal ability. He played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[NRL]]. He was considered a player with natural pace and try scoring ability.

==Background==
Robertson was born in [[Wellington, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
Robertson played junior football with Valley Dragons in the [[Australian Capital Territory|ACT]], and was graded to the [[Canberra Raiders]], making his first-grade début in 2001 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Barry Hildebrandt|work=Wellington Times|title=Big day for Robertson|url=http://wellington.yourguide.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/big-day-for-robertson/679781.aspx|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081011011724/http://wellington.yourguide.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/big-day-for-robertson/679781.aspx|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 October 2008|access-date=2008-09-29|date=28 September 2008}}</ref>
[[File:Michael Robertson.JPG|100px|thumb|right|Robertson in action for the Sea Eagles]]

After signing to play for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] from the start of the [[2006 NRL season]], Robertson cemented his spot on the left win for Manly and played in the [[2007 NRL grand final]] defeat by the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,24415751-5001023,00.html| work=The Daily Telegraph| title=Factfile on 2007 grand final| date=29 September 2008}}</ref>

He scored three tries in the Manly club's record breaking 40-0 [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] victory over Melbourne, becoming the first player to score three tries in an Australian Grand Final since [[Steve Renouf]] did so for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season#Grand final|1997 Super League Grand Final]].<ref name="Grand final: As it happened">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|title=Grand final: As it happened|work=Fox Sports|date=5 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-05|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121204185816/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|archive-date=4 December 2012}}</ref> He would then go on to play in Manly's 28–20 win over English [[Super League XIII|Super League]] champions the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[2009 World Club Challenge]] played at [[Elland Road]] in [[Leeds]].

Normally a left side winger, suspension and injuries to [[Brett Stewart (rugby league)|Brett Stewart]] saw Robertson play most of the [[2009 NRL season]] at fullback for the Sea Eagles. When Stewart suffered a season ending knee injury in Round 1 of the [[2010 NRL season|2010 season]] against the [[Wests Tigers]], it was expected that coach [[Des Hasler]] would move Robertson to fullback for the season. However, the pre-season signing of former [[North Queensland Cowboys]] utility back [[Ben Farrar]] allowed Hasler to keep Robbo in his referred wing position and play Farrar at fullback.

After débuting for the Manly club in round 2, [[2006 NRL season|2006]] Robertson played in 139 consecutive club games until suffering a knee injury in a two try performance against the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in round 10 of the [[2011 NRL season|2011 season]]. The injury was expected to keep Robertson out only for a single game, but he ended up missing three games. He returned to the club in Manly's 22–18 win over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] at Brookvale Oval<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/injury-ends-robbos-streak,-stewart-back/tabid/10874/newsid/62938/default.aspx |title=Injury ends Robbo's streak, Stewart back - NRL.com |website=www.nrl.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121003102626/http://www.nrl.com/injury-ends-robbos-streak,-stewart-back/tabid/10874/newsid/62938/default.aspx |archive-date=2012-10-03}} </ref>

Towards the end of the 2011 NRL home and away season, Robertson announced that he would be leaving Manly after six years with the club. Robertson would fulfill a long-held ambition to play in England when he would join the [[Super League]] club the [[London Broncos]] in 2012. Until then Robertson had vowed he would take his place on the wing with Manly in a bid to win his second NRL premiership with Manly-Warringah,<ref>[http://www.seaeagles.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=43275&title=michael-robertson-to-leave-for-london-harlequins Michael Robertson to join Harlequins in 2012] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402103004/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=43275&title=michael-robertson-to-leave-for-london-harlequins |date=2 April 2012}}</ref> which they achieved by taking out the [[2011 NRL Grand Final]] over the [[New Zealand Warriors]] 24–10.

Robertson's last play for Manly-Warringah was a goal kick after the final siren of the 2011 Grand Final converting captain [[Jamie Lyon]]'s try giving the departing winger a perfect 2/2 goal kicking record in the NRL (having also kicked a goal in Manly's 42–8 win over North Queensland 3 weeks earlier in the Qualifying Final) to go along with his 83 tries from 209 first grade appearances for Canberra and Manly.

==Super League==
Robertson made his first appearance on the wing for the London Broncos on 14 February 2012. He scored a try on début in a 24–34 loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at the [[Twickenham Stoop|Stoop Stadium]] in [[London]].  Robertson signed a 2-year deal midway through the 2012 season.  In total, Robertson made 41 appearances for London scoring 18 tries.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18578386|title=Michael Robertson|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2012-06-26}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Due to his Scottish heritage, Robertson made himself available for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign.<ref name="Robertson ready for Scotland">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/05/29/RUGBYL_Scotland.html |title=Robertson ready for Scotland |publisher=Sportinglife |date=30 May 2008 |access-date=2008-05-30 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605011136/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F08%2F05%2F29%2FRUGBYL_Scotland.html |archive-date=5 June 2011 }}</ref><ref name="Robertson set for kilt call-up">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121206035930/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 December 2012|title=Robertson set for kilt call-up|work=Fox Sports|date=30 May 2008|access-date=2008-05-30}}</ref><ref name="Aussie stars join Scotland">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3628406,00.html|title=Aussie stars join Scotland|publisher=Sky Sports|date=30 May 2008|access-date=2008-05-30}}</ref> Robertson was named in the Scotland squad for the World Cup and played all three of their games at {{rlp|fb}}, scoring a try in the team's 18–16 win over [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at [[Bluetongue Stadium]] in [[Gosford]].<ref name="Logan back in Scotland cup squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7655662.stm|title=Logan back in Scotland cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref>

Robertson's good form to start the [[2010 NRL season]] scoring 7 tries in 8 games in his preferred position of wing was rewarded with selection for [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|Country Origin]] in the annual [[City vs Country Origin]] match in [[Port Macquarie]]. Robertson, who is normally a left winger for the Sea Eagles, played on the right wing and scored a try in Country's 36–18 win over [[New South Wales City rugby league team|City]].
